close_reason: Edge case: empty data map vs missing secret. Needs integration testing to determine correct behavior.
description:
lib.rs:240-247: get() without ?field= rejects an empty data map with DecodeFailed('vault secret has no fields; use ?field=...'). The intent is correct (returning b'{}' would silently give callers an unusable secret), but DecodeFailed is the wrong error variant — the bytes are not malformed, they are well-formed-but-empty.

Trade-off: NotFound is also wrong (the secret exists, just has no fields). Backend is also wrong (Vault is fine). DecodeFailed is the closest fit but is misleading.

Bad behavior: a caller catching DecodeFailed expecting bytes-level corruption gets confused; logs say 'decode failed' for what is actually a configuration / data-shape issue.

Fix options:
1. Live with DecodeFailed and improve the message (current state, marginal improvement only).
2. Add a new SecretError variant (e.g. EmptySecret) — breaking API change, file in secretx-core scope.
3. Treat empty map as NotFound — defensible but loses the distinction between 'no version exists' (404) and 'version exists but empty'.

P3 because the behavior is documented in the get() docstring and the error message guides the user to the fix (?field=).
title: Empty data map rejected as DecodeFailed — ambiguous with valid empty Vault secret
